Class AuthController

java.lang.Object
com.finvision.controller.AuthController
@Controller public class AuthController extends Object
Handles user authentication flows: registration, login, and password reset.

The password-reset flow is a three-step process:

  1. User submits their username (/forgot-password).
  2. User verifies identity via PIN or security question (/verify-identity).
  3. User sets a new password (/reset-password).
The verified username is held in the HTTP session between steps 2 and 3.

  • Constructor Details

    • AuthController

      public AuthController()

  • Method Details

    • root

      @GetMapping("/") public String root()
      Redirects the root URL to the login page.
      Returns:
      redirect to /login

    • login

      @GetMapping("/login") public String login()
      Displays the login page.
      Returns:
      the login template name

    • register

      @GetMapping("/register") public String register()
      Displays the registration page.
      Returns:
      the register template name

    • registerUser

      @PostMapping("/register") public String registerUser(@RequestParam String username, @RequestParam String password, @RequestParam(required=false) String confirmPassword, @RequestParam(required=false) String email, @RequestParam(required=false) String firstName, @RequestParam(required=false) String lastName, @RequestParam(required=false) String pin, @RequestParam(required=false) String securityQuestion, @RequestParam(required=false) String securityAnswer, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Processes the registration form.

      Validates that passwords match, that the username is not already taken, and that the PIN is exactly 4 digits. On success, saves the new user with BCrypt-hashed password and PIN, then redirects to the login page.

      Parameters:
      username - chosen username
      password - chosen password
      confirmPassword - password confirmation (optional)
      email - optional email address
      firstName - optional first name
      lastName - optional last name
      pin - 4-digit numeric PIN for account recovery
      securityQuestion - optional security question for password reset
      securityAnswer - answer to the security question (stored lowercase)
      model - Spring MVC model for error messages
      Returns:
      redirect to /login?registered=true on success, or back to the registration page with an error message

    • showForgotPasswordPage

      @GetMapping("/forgot-password") public String showForgotPasswordPage()
      Displays the "Forgot Password" page (Step 1 of password reset).
      Returns:
      the forgot-password template name

    • processForgotPassword

      @PostMapping("/forgot-password") public String processForgotPassword(@RequestParam("username") String username, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Processes the username submission for password reset (Step 1).

      Looks up the account and, if found, forwards to the reset-password page with the user's security question pre-loaded for Step 2.

      Parameters:
      username - the username whose password should be reset
      model - Spring MVC model
      Returns:
      the reset-password template (Step 2 view) or back to forgot-password with an error if the username is not found

    • verifyIdentity

      @PostMapping("/verify-identity") public String verifyIdentity(@RequestParam String username, @RequestParam String verifyMethod, @RequestParam(required=false) String pinValue, @RequestParam(required=false) String securityAnswer, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest request, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Verifies the user's identity via PIN or security-question answer (Step 2).

      On success, stores the username in the HTTP session under the key pendingReset so Step 3 can authorize the password change without requiring the user to re-verify.

      Parameters:
      username - the username being verified
      verifyMethod - either "pin" or "question"
      pinValue - the 4-digit PIN (required when verifyMethod is "pin")
      securityAnswer - the answer to the security question (required when method is "question")
      request - the HTTP request (used to access the session)
      model - Spring MVC model
      Returns:
      the reset-password template advanced to Step 3 on success, or Step 2 again with an error message on failure

    • resetPasswordPage

      @GetMapping("/reset-password") public String resetPasswordPage(jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest request, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Displays the reset-password page. If a pendingReset session attribute is present the page renders in Step 3 (new-password entry) mode.
      Parameters:
      request - the HTTP request (checked for the pendingReset session attribute)
      model - Spring MVC model
      Returns:
      the reset-password template

    • processResetPassword

      @PostMapping("/reset-password") public String processResetPassword(@RequestParam String newPassword, @RequestParam(required=false) String confirmPassword, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest request, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Saves the new password for the user identified by the pendingReset session attribute (Step 3).

      Validates that the new password is confirmed and at least 6 characters long, then BCrypt-hashes and persists it. Clears the session attribute on success.

      Parameters:
      newPassword - the new password entered by the user
      confirmPassword - optional confirmation of the new password
      request - HTTP request used to access and clear the session
      model - Spring MVC model
      Returns:
      redirects to /forgot-password if the session is missing; otherwise renders the login page with a success message or the reset page with an error
